title: Reader Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Static Architecture, and Evidence · Runtime Lens: Accessibility Charter
summary: A team says “build a blog generator” but has not defined readers, publishing authority, content ownership, supported routes, accessibility needs, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the accessibility charter increment in reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter. Learners progress thro…
objectives: Record language, reading, keyboard, reflow, motion, media, cognitive, and assistive-technology needs as build inputs.
Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Accessibility Charter · Theory Accessibility Charter
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 8737 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record language, reading, keyboard, reflow, motion, media, cognitive, and assistive-technology needs as build inputs.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Accessibility Charter contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example publishing team handles case 8737 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Accessibility Charter, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-outcomes-repo-architecture-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-outcomes-repo-architecture-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_outcomes_repo_architecture_2(reader_task: str = "find-current-guidance", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, content_authorized: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if not reader_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not content_authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "content-authority"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-site-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Accessibility Charter evidence · Theory Accessibility Chart…
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-accessibility-charter-kxvsp" that uses case 1467 to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Build Accessibility Charter evidence · Theory Accessibility Chart….

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-accessibility-charter-kxvsp
# Competency: ssg-accessibility-charter.
# Case 1467: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_accessibility_charter_kxvsp(reader_task: str = "find-current-guidance", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, content_authorized: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_kxvsp = "kxvsp-psvxk-1467"
    assert evidence_variant_kxvsp
    evidence_axes_kxvsp = (
        "readerkxvsp",
        "source1467",
        "parsepsvxk",
        "routekxvsp1467",
        "artifact1467kxvsp",
        "recoverypsvxk1467",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_kxvsp)) == 6
    if not reader_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not content_authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "content-authority"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-site-charter"

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record language, reading, keyboard, reflow, motion, media, cognitive, and assistive-technology needs as build inputs.

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the accessibility charter defect evidence boundary.

For repair the accessibility charter defect, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Accessibility Charter, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Cha…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Accessibility Charter: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 1547 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record language, reading, keyboard, reflow, motion, media, cognitive, and assistive-technology needs as build inputs.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Cha….
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Cha…, keep Accessibility Charter connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record language, reading, keyboard, reflow, motion, media, cognitive, and assistive-technology needs as build inputs.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Cha…, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Accessibility Charter precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Cha…, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 1547 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record language, reading, keyboard, reflow, motion, media, cognitive, and assistive-technology needs as build inputs.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Cha…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Accessibility can be added to templates after content and routes are finished.
